sources_exist.note: Nine URL occurrences resolved, but both buy_mult and exit_mult occurrences point to the same page that returned an internal error.

figures_match_sources.note: Ten of eleven audits fail claim-level support. Key failures include an incomplete null-fallback n_band chain, an arbitrary conversion from 63% any-use to 30% material AI use, unsupported prior-technology timing and fee data, proxy owner demographics, overbroad consolidator scope, and inaccessible multiple evidence.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The bounded role fractions and broad PRESSURED thesis are plausible, but t50_years=2.5 rests on an unsupported 30% material-adoption estimate and unsourced 5-10-year analog; pi_dist and pi_moat also rely on fee, retention, assignment, and fiduciary claims not established by their cited pages.

rubric_consistent.note: The arithmetic is exact, heterogeneous treatment is explicit, and deal evidence is confined to B/M. However, the authorized n_band null fallback is incomplete and internally inconsistent: the chain uses a 27.5% margin while margin_used remains 11.65%, and the 15% AUM-band share, 0.75% fee, 27.5% margin, and 0.66 wealth scalar lack auditable URLs. The active-consolidator count also includes employee-owned and explicitly non-PE buyers.

cross_checks_honest.note: All four cross-checks are present and no-proxy separation is explicit, but terminal_value and pricing_model_deflation repeat unsourced 25-30bp robo pricing, 0.7-1.0% realized fees, assignment friction, and client-retention assertions.

source_audits.note: COMPLY/IAA supports 16,544 SEC advisers, 67.4% below $1B, 87.3% below $5B, and individual-focused firms averaging 8 employees/$424M AUM. It does not support the added 15% $500M-$1B share, 0.75% fee, 27.5% margin, or 0.66 wealth scalar that drive n_band=3,800.

The guide presents four buyer tiers and extensive dated activity, but the record's 32-name count includes employee-owned firms and a tier explicitly labeled non-PE buyers; it therefore does not establish 32 PE-backed serial acquirers active in the prior 24 months.

The cited page returned an internal error. Search indexing showed only broader alternative tier summaries, not inspectable support for the quoted 7.5x-10.0x range, 9.0x median, or adjusted-EBITDA convention.

Schwab supports 63% using AI in some capacity, early-stage administrative use, and only about one in ten AI users fully integrating it. It does not measure 30% material production use; taking roughly half of 63% conflicts with the page's much smaller fully integrated share.
The cited page returned an internal error and could not be inspected for the asserted $3B-$20B 15x-19x EBITDA tier, 21x premium range, or suitability of 17x for the modeled platform.

The accessible benchmarking article discusses 2024 growth, client experience, and AI use cases but contains no e-signature, planning-software, or CRM diffusion timeline and does not support the asserted 5-10 or 10-15 years to majority adoption.

The 2022 release states 37% of advisors controlling 40% of assets are expected to retire within ten years and one in four lacks a settled succession plan. It does not report 42% of headcount aged 55+, 57% of assets, 37.4%/105,887, $2.5T, or a 60+ owner share supporting value 0.35.

The page says the study covers products/pricing and reports growth and AI-use findings, but publishes no realized AUM fee rate, fee-rate stability, assignment-consent data, book portability, or robo-pricing evidence used in pi_dist.

The guide explicitly treats AUM-based fees as recurring revenue, describes retention thresholds and founder-dependency risk, and states AUM times blended fee equals revenue, supporting the relationship-capture and cost-decoupled pricing rationale.

The Cerulli release concerns advisor retirement and succession preferences; it does not establish 95%+ client retention, fiduciary/regulatory barriers, robo-advisor displacement limits, or conversational-AI bypass risk used to set pi_moat.
The public Schwab article contains no fee data and does not establish dominant AUM billing, a 0.7-1.0% realized rate, minority subscription/hourly models, or performance-fee prevalence.

reasons: dataset_inputs.n_band.derivation (https://www.comply.com/resource/2026-investment-adviser-industry-snapshot-shows-continued-growth-in-demand-for-adviser-services/) does not provide a complete null-fallback research chain: add auditable sources for the $500M-$1B share, realized fee, operating margin, and wealth-firm scalar, and make margin_used/margin_source agree with the margin actually applied.

inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260122602006/en/Schwab-Study-Reveals-RIA-AI-Adoption-More-Than-Doubles---But-Most-Firms-Still-in-Early-Stages) measures 63% any use and about 6.3% fully integrated use, not 30% material use; source a production-workflow adoption measure or derive an explicitly labeled estimate from disclosed use-case shares.

inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://advisorservices.schwab.com/insights-hub/perspectives/ria-benchmarking-study-2025) contains no prior-technology diffusion timeline; cite dated start and majority-adoption evidence for each analog before deriving t50_years.

inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://www.cerulli.com/press-releases/40-of-advisory-assets-will-transition-in-10-years-according-to-cerulli) supports retirement/succession pressure but not value 0.35 or most quoted demographics; obtain an owner/principal age distribution or use only the source's measured retirement cohort with a transparent conversion.

inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/who-buys-rias-wealth-management-2026/) includes employee-owned and explicitly non-PE buyers in the 32 names; re-count only PE-backed platforms with documented qualifying add-ons during the required 24-month window.

inputs.buy_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/ria-wealth-management-ma-multiples-2026/) is inaccessible and does not auditably support 9.0x; cite the named tracker report or another accessible source reporting the small-firm adjusted-EBITDA median after owner compensation.

inputs.exit_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/ria-wealth-management-ma-multiples-2026/) is inaccessible and does not auditably support 17.0x; cite an accessible size-tier tracker or platform transaction/comparable supporting the selected $10M+ EBITDA exit multiple.

inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://advisorservices.schwab.com/insights-hub/perspectives/ria-benchmarking-study-2025) publishes no fee figures; cite accessible fee-structure and realized-rate evidence for dominant AUM billing and the minority models.

inputs.pi_dist.sources[0] (https://www.aboutschwab.com/ria-benchmarking-study-2025) and inputs.pi_moat.sources[0] (https://www.cerulli.com/press-releases/40-of-advisory-assets-will-transition-in-10-years-according-to-cerulli) do not support the asserted fee stability, assignment friction, fiduciary moat, or client-retention claims; replace or supplement them with regulatory and retention/fee benchmarks tied to those mechanisms.
